critic_raw:
Issue 1: Conflation of prognosis and treatment benefit. The study measures progression-free survival under historical treatment. This is a prognostic model. A therapy-guidance system must predict differential treatment benefit — i.e., whether patient A would do better on regimen X versus regimen Y. The study does not estimate treatment effect heterogeneity. The slide's claim is a category error.

Issue 2: Single-tumor-type evidence applied to all solid tumors. The study covers only early-stage breast cancer. Different solid tumors have different tumor microenvironments, genomic drivers, histopathological patterns relevant to prognosis, and standard treatment options. The claim that this model can guide therapy across solid tumors is unsupported — each tumor type requires independent validation.

Issue 3: Single-institution validation scope. One hospital's pathology workflow does not represent the distribution of institutional workflows. Scanner calibration, staining protocols, and slide preparation vary. The model may not transfer without retraining or recalibration at other centers.

Issue 4: Retrospective treatment confounding for causal claims. Clinician-selected treatment over ten years reflects selection on many unobserved factors. Image features correlated with outcomes in this dataset may be proxies for those selection factors rather than for treatment-effect mechanisms.
defender_raw: The four issues are all valid. The slide's claim must be corrected. The defensible position is: this model shows prognostic enrichment value beyond standard covariates in early-stage breast cancer at one institution; its clinical utility needs prospective validation; no therapy-selection or cross-tumor claims are currently supported. Whole-slide imaging is not inherently an invalid modality — the issue is the scope of the current claim, not the approach.
adjudication_raw: Immediate convergence — the defender concedes all four issues and provides a corrected narrower claim. Critique wins on the specific question of whether the draft slide claim is supported.
all_issues_raised: Issue 1: prognosis vs. therapy-guidance category error

empirical_test.condition: Multi-center prospective treatment-benefit heterogeneity studies within each claimed tumor type
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: No differential treatment-benefit prediction; poor cross-tumor or cross-center transfer
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Treatment-benefit heterogeneity demonstrated prospectively across claimed contexts
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Prognostic value confirmed broadly; no demonstrated therapy-selection value
